title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
summary: Google Cloud presented integration methods for Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) that standardize agent connections to data sources like BigQuery, AlloyDB, and Google Maps. The session covered AgentOps for observability into agent performance and cost metrics.
writeup: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) standardize the connections between agents and data sources, including BigQuery, AlloyDB, and Google Maps. The session frames MCP as a common interface for tool calling, reducing the custom integration work needed to wire agents to backend systems. It also covers AgentOps, which provides observability into agent performance and cost metrics. The combined focus is building agents and operating them with visibility into how they run and what they cost.
